ongoing [/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][/et_pb_row][/et_pb_section][et_pb_section fb_built=”1″ admin_label=”Project Details” _builder_version=”4.16″ custom_padding=”90px||90px||true” global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_row column_structure=”1_2,1_2″ _builder_version=”4.16″ global_colors_info=”{}”][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.16″ custom_padding=”|||” global_colors_info=”{}” custom_padding__hover=”|||”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.4″ text_font=”||||||||” link_font=”||||||||” ul_font=”||||||||” ol_font=”||||||||” quote_font=”||||||||” header_font=”||||||||” header_2_font=”Outfit|700|||||||” header_2_font_size=”42px” header_2_line_height=”1.2em” module_alignment=”center” custom_margin=”|||” custom_padding=”|||” header_2_font_size_tablet=”” header_2_font_size_phone=”36px” header_2_font_size_last_edited=”on|phone” locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{}”] About this Project [/et_pb_text][et_pb_divider color=”#ffb400″ divider_position=”center” divider_weight=”5px” _builder_version=”4.16″ max_width=”50px” locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{}”][/et_pb_divider][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.4″ text_font=”||||||||” custom_padding=”|||” custom_padding_tablet=”|40px||40px||true” custom_padding_last_edited=”off|desktop” locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{}”] Whitefish Lake First Nation #128 sponsored the extension of its sustainable potable water supply system to provide direct service connections to multiple residents and the abandonment of aging cistern services. This phase of the works include the installation of a HDPE main extension using horizontal directional drilling (HDD). Construction particulars include Project start up activities; contract execution, submittals (ECO/ESC Plans, TAS, schedule, shop drawings), staged procurement HDD installation of 250mm (6.6km) and 150mm (1.3km) HDPE HDD installation of 40mm (600m) metered residential services (17 in total) with accompanying cistern abandonments Installation of direct bury air releases (2), gate valves (25), and fire hydrants (17), one blow off Tie-in to the existing watermain infrastructure at two locations and commissioning activities (hydrostatic testing, disinfection and swabbing) Roadway and landscape restoration Pending extra work consists of Two new commercial service and one residential service connections Tie-in for seven (7) existing residential services to the mainline Tie-in for three (3) cistern/home connections [/et_pb_text][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.4″ _module_preset=”default” global_colors_info=”{}”] CONSTRUCTION TEAM Johan Kruithof, General Manager Steven Beath, Superintendent Brad Buffalo, Construction Manager Dana Sutherland, Foreman Brandy Braun, Project Manager [/et_pb_text][/et_pb_column][et_pb_column type=”1_2″ _builder_version=”4.16″ custom_padding=”|||” global_colors_info=”{}” custom_padding__hover=”|||”][et_pb_text _builder_version=”4.27.4″ text_font=”||||||||” custom_padding=”|||” custom_padding_tablet=”|40px||40px||true” custom_padding_last_edited=”off|desktop” locked=”off” global_colors_info=”{}”] LOCATION Goodfish Lake, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION August 2025 - ongoing PROJECT BUDGET $4,247,000 Extra work pending – value TBD FINAL PROJECT VALUE TBD CLIENT Whitefish Lake First Nation #128 Marv Steinhauer, 780.227.1240 msteinhauer@wfl128.ca CONSULTANT Associated Engineering Ryan Krausher, 780.920.6588 krausherr@ae.ca KEY FIRM Option Excavating Inc. title: O’Chiese Education Way Subdivision
headings: H2: Let's start your project today
text: O’Chiese Education Way Subdivision LOCATION O’Chiese, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION August 2025 - ongoing About this Project The Education Way Subdivision was sponsored by the O’Chiese First Nation to expand the number of residential homes on the Nation. Option was engaged by the General Contractor to install the water and sanitary mains, and home connections in advance of roadway and lot preparation. Construction particulars included Project start up activities; subcontract execution, submittals (Schedule and Shop Drawings), staged procurement Open cut installation of 200mm (1km) and 150mm (300m) of waterline, up to 5m in depth Open cut installation of 100mm (500m), 75mm (500m) and 50mm (300m) of low pressure sanitary main Installation of sanitary clean outs - 100mm (2), 75mm (3) and 50mm (4) 42 residential water service and sanitary service connections with integrated sanitary grinder pump packages Tie-ins to the existing infrastructure, hydrostatic testing, disinfection and swabbing & flushing of the waterline CONSTRUCTION TEAM Johan Kruithof, General Manager Steven Beath, Superintendent Brad Buffalo, Construction Manager Matt Giese, Foreman Brandy Braun, Project Manager LOCATION O’Chiese, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION August 2025 - ongoing PROJECT BUDGET $2,589,000 FINAL PROJECT VALUE TBD GENERAL CONTRACTOR Prentice Creek Contracting Ltd. Kelvin Wiley, 825.733.0367 Kwiley.pccl@gmail.com CONSULTANT Urban Systems Benjamin Corenblum, 780.777.9009 bcorenblum@urbansystems.ca KEY FIRM Option Excavating Inc. Let's start your project today Let's turn your dream project into a reality! With our expertise and passion, we are committed to bringing your ideas to life. title: KCN Water Distribution Line - Phase 2
headings: H1: KCN Water Distribution Line - Phase 2
H2: Let's start your project today
text: KCN Water Distribution Line - Phase 2 LOCATION Kehewin, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION October 2023 - November 2025 About this Project Kehewin Cree Nation sponsored an upgrade the existing water treatment plan and provide water services to 68 residences. Option worked with the Nation to find cost savings of $2.5 million, providing an opportunity to expand the project scope to include additional phases (2B, 2A and Hwy 41). Option was also able to participate in the pilot program for Pehta, a new financial reporting tool for First Nations. Construction particulars included, over an above landscape restoration Laydown and site preparation (clearing, grubbing and electrification) 200mm water main installation (10.5km) via HDD and two tie ins to the existing infrastructure Upgrades to the water treatment plan, including distribution pump system upgrade 68 residential service connections of 40mm pipe via HDD, one trickle feed system installation, the decommission of 70 residential cisterns Installation of air release valves (17), gate valves (13), and blow offs (5) Extra work consisted of Ph 2B - 200mm water main installation via HDD (2.7km) Service connections via 40mm pipe via HDD; Ph 2B - 23 connections (2km) and decommission of 24 cisterns, Ph 2A - 11 service connections (1km) and decommission of 12 cisterns, Hwy 41 - eight service connections (1km), four cistern decommissions and four trickle feed system installations Ph 2B installation of air release valves (2), gate valves (3), and one blow off CONSTRUCTION TEAM Johan Kruithof, General Manager Steven Beath, Superintendent Brad Buffalo, Construction Manager Albert (Albie) Kavanaugh, Foreman Brandy Braun, Project Manager Dana Sutherland, Foreman LOCATION Kehewin, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION October 2023 - November 2025 PROJECT BUDGET $10,953,000 Extra work $ 3,316,000 FINAL PROJECT VALUE $11,770,000 CLIENT Kehewin Cree Nation #123 Denise Hourd, 587.201.6411 denise@kehewin.ca CONSULTANT WSP Canada Inc. Gorin Gajic, 780.951.4080 gorin.gajic@wsp.com KEY FIRM Option Excavating Inc. Let's start your project today Let's turn your dream project into a reality! With our expertise and passion, we are committed to bringing your ideas to life. Contact us today and let's get started on making your vision a tangible success. Together, we can create something extraordinary. Contact Us

title: 2024 Storm Drainage & Outfall Repair
headings: H2: Let's start your project today
text: 2024 Storm Drainage & Outfall Repair LOCATION Grande Prairie, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION August - December, 2024 Spring work pending About this Project This project, sponsored by the City of Grande Prairie, consisted of the installation/replacement of PVC storm sewer lines to repair distressed infrastructure, outfall improvements, asphalt restoration (urban and trail locations) and landscape restoration, at seventeen locations throughout the city. Extra work was requested by The City to address additional repairs in a residential neighbourhood to due to the deterioration of the infrastructure and address other field changes needed at multiple locations. Construction particulars included Project start up activities; contract execution, submittals (ECO/ESC Plan, TAS, Schedule, Shop Drawings, Permitting), and procurement Installation of 300/375/450/525/750/1050mm PVC storm sewer (366m, total) c/w fittings, manholes, catch basins and CCTV inspection 450mm flared end and 750mm headwall replacements Installation of 300/400mm CSP culvert (30m, total) with rip rap end treatments Asphalt pavement restoration (3,150m2), urban/trail landscape restoration CONSTRUCTION TEAM Johan Kruithof, General Manager Brad Buffalo, Construction Manager Brandy Braun, Project Manager Albert (Albie) Kavanaugh, Foreman LOCATION Grande Prairie, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION August - December, 2024 Spring work pending PROJECT BUDGET $2,207,000 Extra work $160,000 FINAL PROJECT VALUE TBD CLIENT City of Grande Prairie Alissa Hodson, C.E.T., 780.513.5234 ahodson@cityofgp.com CONSULTANT McElhanney Kyler Lucas, C.E.T., 250.300.3301 klucas@mcelhanney.com KEY FIRM Option Excavating Inc. Let's start your project today Let's turn your dream project into a reality! With our expertise and passion, we are committed to bringing your ideas to life. Contact us today and let's get started on making your vision a tangible success. Together, we can create something extraordinary. Contact Us

title: Calahoo Road Storm Sewer
headings: H2: Let's start your project today
text: Calahoo Road Storm Sewer LOCATION Spruce Grove, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION May, 2024 About this Project This project, sponsored by the City of Spruce Grove, comprised of a coordinated partial closure of Calahoo Road to address storm water drainage issues in the area by adding needed infrastructure. Construction particulars included Project start up activities; contract execution, submittals and procurement Traffic control measures establishment and management of changes as work progressed Saw cutting and asphalt/roadway removals Installation of 23m of 300mm DR 35 storm sewer pipe with fillcrete Installation of a 1200mm storm manhole A tie into the existing infrastructure Roadway preparation and asphalt restoration Restoration of curb and sidewalk CONSTRUCTION TEAM Johan Kruithof, General Manager Brad Buffalo, Construction Manager Brandy Braun, Project Manager Albert (Albie) Kavanaugh, Foreman LOCATION Spruce Grove, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION May, 2024 PROJECT BUDGET $114,450 FINAL PROJECT VALUE $114,450 CLIENT City of Spruce Grove Trevor Crawford, CET, 780.962.7620 tcrawford@sprucegrove.org CONSULTANT N/A KEY FIRM Option Excavating Inc. title: Clearwater Sunset Dr. Sewer Main Repairs
headings: H1: Clearwater Sunset Dr. Sewer Main Repairs
H2: Let's start your project today
text: Clearwater Sunset Dr. Sewer Main Repairs LOCATION Clearwater River, Saskatchewan CONSTRUCTION DURATION April - September, 2023 About this Project The Clearwater River Dene Nation sponsored this project located in Clearwater River, Saskatchewan - a remote community approximately 600kms north of Saskatoon, Saskatchewan. Work is comprised of the repair of underground utilities, reinstallation of a service connection and roadway repairs. While on site The Nation took the opportunity to address an additional in sanitary repairs. Construction particulars include 200mm (111m) storm sewer line replacement between manholes Manhole repairs, one lowered and six requiring adjustments and repairs Ditch repairs and installation of erosion control measures including foundation improvements and rip rap installation Youth Center sanitary sewer building service repairs 150mm insulated pipe (34m) with 200mm duct and associated service connection Extra work included; a residential service replacement and additional sanitary repairs in multiple locations Site restoration of roadways including subgrade preparation, traffic gravel placement, CSP culvert installations and driveway approach installations CONSTRUCTION TEAM Johan Kruithof, General Manager Brad Buffalo, Superintendent Brandy Braun, Project Manager Steven Beath, Sr. Foreman LOCATION Clearwater River, Saskatchewan CONSTRUCTION DURATION April - September, 2023 PROJECT BUDGET $365,000 Extra work $47,000 FINAL PROJECT VALUE $412,000 CLIENT Indigenous Relations Project Management (IR) Grant McKercher, 639.471.4755 grant.mckercher@irprojectmanagement.ca CONSULTANT AECOM Canada Ltd. title: Acheson Zone 5 Wastewater Trunk Upgrades
headings: H1: Acheson Zone 5 Wastewater Trunk Upgrades
H2: Let's start your project today
text: Acheson Zone 5 Wastewater Trunk Upgrades LOCATION Acheson, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION July - November 8, 2024 About this Project This project, sponsored by the Parkland County, comprised of the removal and replacement of the existing gravity sanitary mains and the installation of a sanitary force main in Acheson, AB. While on site the County took the opportunity to address additional roadway improvements. Construction particulars included Project start up activities; contract execution, submittals (ECO/ESC Plan, TAS, Schedule, Shop Drawings), and procurement 250mm (45m), 300mm (25m), and 525mm PVC (175m) gravity sanitary sewer main installation via open cut, depths of 3m to 8m 450mm HDPE sanitary forcemain installation; 375m via HDD and 195m via open cut 1200mm sanitary manholes (20 vm) Existing lift station improvement; connection of 300mm PVC sanitary into lift station wet well via coring and LinkSeal, installation of a deflector chute assembly, modification to the safety platform, chain link fence installation and culvert installation with end treatments. Roadway restoration including granular base course, cement stabilization (3,735m2), asphalt restoration (2,520m2) and additional roadwork (3,695m2) CONSTRUCTION TEAM Johan Kruithof, General Manager Steven Beath, Superintendent Brad Buffalo, Construction Manager Albert Kavanaugh, Foreman Brandy Braun, Project Manager LOCATION Acheson, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION July - November 8, 2024 PROJECT BUDGET $2,220,000 Extra work $76,000 FINAL PROJECT VALUE $2,296,000 CLIENT Parkland County Keith Bobey, P. Eng, 780.974.0061 keith.bobey@parklandcounty.com CONSULTANT Select Engineering Consultants Justin Young, P. title: 51St Street Storm Sewer Replacement
headings: H2: Let's start your project today
text: 51St Street Storm Sewer Replacement LOCATION Edson, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION September - October, 2022 About this Project The Town sponsored the replacement of a collapsed and damaged section of storm piping on 51 Street in Edson, which straddles the east and westbound lanes of the Yellowhead Highway (Hwy 16). Due to year-end financial constraints, Option collaborated with The Town to adjust the project scope and find cost saving alternatives to reduce the project budget by $112,000. A further $24,000 of savings were found during construction to assist the Town and successfully deliver the project under budget. Construction particulars included Project start up activities; contract execution, submittals (ECO/ESC Plan, TAS, Schedule, Shop Drawings), and procurement Traffic accommodation measures addressing heavy volume traffic on Hwy 16 New storm sewer installation of 600mm CL4 (96m), 1500mm manholes (2), 900mm catch basins (3) and connection leads to the existing infrastructure New wheelchair/bike ramps and the restoration of 40m of curb and gutter Restoration of 1300m2 of asphalt Site restoration CONSTRUCTION TEAM Johan Kruithof, General Manager Brad Buffalo, Superintendent Brandy Braun, Project Manager Steven Beath, Foreman LOCATION Edson, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION September - October, 2022 PROJECT BUDGET $584,000 FINAL PROJECT VALUE $560,000 CLIENT Town of Edson Pat Fisher, 403.230.1401 patf@edson.ca CONSULTANT WSP Canada Inc. Michael Steffler P.Eng., 780.712.5000 michael.steffler@wsp.com KEY FIRM Option Excavating Inc. Let's start your project today Let's turn your dream project into a reality! title: Ryley 2024 Capital Works
headings: H2: Let's start your project today
text: Ryley 2024 Capital Works LOCATION Ryley, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION September - November, 2024 About this Project Sponsored by the Village of Ryley, work consisted of the replacement of sanitary and water mains, and site restoration in Ryley, Alberta. Extra work was requested during construction to extend the temporary water services and revise two facility service connections to accommodate public use, Option collaborated with the Village to find costs savings during construction to lessen the impact to the total budget. Construction particulars include Project start up activities; contract execution, submittals (ECO/ESC Plan, TAS, Schedule, Shop Drawings), and procurement Removals including the existing road structure (2,900m2), the existing sanitary sewer main and water main (asbestos cement), sidewalk, curb and gutter, concrete stair, and misc. other removals Installation of 200mm PVC SDR35 sanitary sewer main (235m) with portions insulated and the replacement of three sanitary manholes Installation of 150mm PVC DR18 watermain (245m) with three tie ins to the existing infrastructure Replacement of 10 residential service connections (water and sanitary) Asphalt pavement restoration (245m2) Site restoration CONSTRUCTION TEAM Johan Kruithof, General Manager Steven Beath, Superintendent Brad Buffalo, Construction Manager Albert Kavanaugh, Foreman Brandy Braun, Project Manager LOCATION Ryley, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION September - November, 2024 PROJECT BUDGET $700,500 Extra work $22,000 FINAL PROJECT VALUE $711,000 CLIENT Village of Ryley Glen Hamilton-Brown, 780.663.3653 cao@ryley.ca CONSULTANT MPE Engineering Mike Dowhun, C. title: Lymburn Esso Station Remediation
headings: H2: Let's start your project today
text: Lymburn Esso Station Remediation LOCATION Edmonton, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION September and November, 2022 About this Project This project was privately negotiated work associated with the decommissioning of a gas services station in Edmonton, Alberta. Construction consisted of two phases to accommodate site and structural works outside of Option’s scope. During the initial phase of work, it became apparent that further removals would be needed. Option collaborated with the client to address the additional work within the project’s scheduling constraints. Phase One included TAS establishment Electrical disconnects and degassing of associated lines Removals consisting of the station canopy, service pumps and tanks including sorting of all removals for disposal/recycling Phase Two included Extra work as requested by the client to address further removals and additional excavation Contaminated material removal Clean fill replacement and backfill CONSTRUCTION TEAM Johan Kruithof, General Manager Brad Buffalo, Superintendent Brandy Braun, Project Manager Steven Beath, Foreman LOCATION Edmonton, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION September and November, 2022 PROJECT BUDGET $135,500 Extra work $24,000 FINAL PROJECT VALUE $159,500 CLIENT Shamrock Property Management Ltd. CONSULTANT Long Chain Reclaim Ltd. (LCR) Myles Ethier, 587.377.7654 methier@lcreclaim.com KEY FIRM Option Excavating Inc. Let's start your project today Let's turn your dream project into a reality! title: Central Peace Regional Water
headings: H2: Let's start your project today
text: Central Peace Regional Water LOCATION Saddle Hill County, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION December 2024 - ongoing About this Project Saddle Hills County sponsored this contract, in conjunction with multiple neighbouring municipalities, taking a substantial step forward in creating a reliable long-term regional water system. This portion of the project saw the traversing of the Dunvegan Creek valley slope (190m vertical elevation) with steel and HDPE water lines and accompanying mechanical installations. Construction particulars included Project start up activities; contract execution, submittals (ECO/ESC and CoW Plans, TAS, Schedule, Shop Drawings), stagged procurement HDD installation of 1.8km 400mm steel waterline, with an additional 45m of open cut installation Open cut installation of 866m of 500mm HDPE waterline Installation of 400mm pigging chambers (2), 500mm pig launch, and 500mm direct bury gate valve Each project end was prepared for a future phases of construction Roadway and landscape restoration CONSTRUCTION TEAM Johan Kruithof, General Manager Steven Beath, Superintendent Brad Buffalo, Construction Manager Matt Giese, Foreman Brandy Braun, Project Manager Albert Kavanaugh, Foreman LOCATION Saddle Hill County, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION December 2024 - ongoing PROJECT BUDGET $14,015,000 FINAL PROJECT VALUE TBD CLIENT Saddle Hills County Ron Pelensky, Director, 780.864.3760 rpelensky@saddlehills.ab.ca CONSULTANT MPE a division of Englobe Haj Oshiki, P.Eng., 780.486.2000 hoshiki@mpe.ca KEY FIRM Option Excavating Inc. title: Spruce Grove Industrial Park - Deep Utilities
headings: H1: Spruce Grove Industrial Park - Deep Utilities
H2: Let's start your project today
text: Spruce Grove Industrial Park - Deep Utilities LOCATION Spruce Grove, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION October - November, 2022 About this Project This project, sponsored by the City of Spruce Grove, comprised the installation of sanitary and water services in preparation for the sale of a block of industrial lots. Option collaborated with The City to find $84,000 in cost savings for a revised budget to align the project with year-end financial constraints. Construction particulars included Project start up activities; contract execution, submittals (ECO/ESC Plan, TAS, Schedule, Shop Drawings), and procurement Deep sanitary installation of 300m of 200mm PVE with 25m, case bored and up to 8m deep, 1200mm manholes (4, 22 vm) and the connection to the existing infrastructure Watermain installation of 250mm (195m), TVS/hot taps (2), fire hydrants with leads (2), connection to the existing infrastructure and preparation for a future connection Installation required threading services between two Trans Mountain /Kinder Morgan Pipelines Restoration of curb/gutter and asphalt Site restoration CONSTRUCTION TEAM Johan Kruithof, General Manager Brad Buffalo, Superintendent Brandy Braun, Project Manager Steven Beath, Foreman LOCATION Spruce Grove, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION October - November, 2022 PROJECT BUDGET $697,000 FINAL PROJECT VALUE $613,000 CLIENT City of Spruce Grove Rae-Lynne Spila P.Eng., 780.962.0651 rspila@sprucegrove.org CONSULTANT Scheffer Andrew Ltd. Patrick Hallonquist, P. Eng., 780.732.7800 p.hallonquist@schefferandrew.com KEY FIRM Option Excavating Inc. Let's start your project today Let's turn your dream project into a reality! With our expertise and passion, we are committed to bringing your ideas to life. Contact us today and let's get started on making your vision a tangible success. Together, we can create something extraordinary. Contact Us

title: Clearwater Stage IV Phase B Subdivision
headings: H1: Clearwater Stage IV Phase B Subdivision
H2: Let's start your project today
text: Clearwater Stage IV Phase B Subdivision LOCATION Clearwater River, Saskatchewan CONSTRUCTION DURATION April - November, 2023 About this Project This subdivision project was sponsored by the Clearwater River Dene Nation, a community located in Clearwater River, Saskatchewan - 600kms north of Saskatoon, Saskatchewan. Work expanded the community by providing water and sanitary services to 33 new residences. Option collaborated with IR to find cost saving alternatives of $45,000 to assist delivering the project under budget. Construction particulars include 1.9 ha of site preparation including clearing, grubbing, tree removals, drainage work cut/fills 200mm sanitary sewer installation (700m), depths of 3 to 4.5m 26 vm manhole installations and connections to existing infrastructure 150mm water main installation (1,400m), commissioning and connections to the existing infrastructure 33 service connections; 855m of sanitary and 805m of water 6,250m2 of roadway work including erosion control and aggregate installations 15,000m3 of common excavation and contractor supplied borrow to complete lot grading and drainage improvements Roadway earthwork for drainage including ditch installations, 400mm CSP culverts (48m)/end treatments and existing ditch improvements CONSTRUCTION TEAM Johan Kruithof, General Manager Brad Buffalo, Construction Manager/Superintendent Brandy Braun, Project Manager Steven Beath, Sr. Foreman Mark Forsey, Jr. Foreman LOCATION Clearwater River, Saskatchewan CONSTRUCTION DURATION April - November, 2023 PROJECT BUDGET $3,157,000 FINAL PROJECT VALUE $3,112,000 CLIENT Indigenous Relations Project Management (IR) Grant McKercher, 639.471.4755 grant.mckercher@irprojectmanagement.ca CONSULTANT AECOM Canada Ltd. Neilie Smith, P.Eng., 639.638.8150 neilie.smith@aecom.com KEY FIRM Option Excavating Inc. Let's start your project today Let's turn your dream project into a reality! title: ACE Water Transmission Main
headings: H2: Let's start your project today
text: ACE Water Transmission Main LOCATION County of Vermilion River, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION March - September 6, 2023 About this Project This project, sponsored by the Alberta Central East (ACE) Water Corporation, comprised the installation of water transmission line from Paradise Valley to Range Road 24, comprising 17kms of directionally drilled line and associated residential service connections. Construction particulars included Project start up activities; contract execution, submittals (ECO / ESC Plan, TAS, Schedule, Shop Drawings), and procurement Horizontal directionally drilled HDPE DR11 100mm (17.14kms) with 200mm HDPE DR 9 casing (925m) Service materials; blow-offs (12), gate valves (2) and air releases (16) Pig launching/retrieval stations (2) Waterline commissioning Connection to the existing infrastructure and preparation for a future phase Site restoration (15,000m2) CONSTRUCTION TEAM Johan Kruithof, General Manager Richard Hrupchuk, Superintendent Brandy Braun, Project Manager Brad Buffalo, Superintendent Steven Beath, Sr. Foreman LOCATION County of Vermilion River, Alberta CONSTRUCTION DURATION March - September 6, 2023 $2,978,000 FINAL PROJECT VALUE $3,035,000 CLIENT Alberta Central East (ACE) Water Corporation CONSULTANT MPE Engineering Ltd. text: Crew Members Our Team Meet the team Our Team Johan Kruithof General Manager Jessie Lecocq Operations Manager Brad Buffalo Civil Construction Manager Brandy Braun Civil Project Manager Ashley Trembley HSE & HR Manager Steven Beath Underground Superintendent Tyler Cust Transportation Manager Meet the team Our Field Team Beyond our experienced management team, we have a dedicated team of field members that do the hard work of actually constructing these projects. As part of our CORE Values we strive to acknowledge the team we are blessed to have, while giving them to tools and equipment to complete work safely. From our crew BBQ’s to our monthly safety rewards program, we try our best to bless and reward them for their long and hard days, as they help us to Build Western Canada, one pipe at a time. Without our team of foreman, operators, pipelayers, and labourers who spend long days out of town and away from their families, there would be no OpEx and there would be no happy clients. title: Services
headings: H3: Lift Stations, Pump Stations & Mechanical
H2: Let's start your project today
text: What We Do Services Subdivisions and land development We take pride in constructing communities, often where we live and work. Our experienced construction team, backed with the right equipment, allows us to execute a wide variety and scale of project – any size, type and depth of pipe can be addressed,from deep trunk lines to large diameter concrete storm and box culverts, water distribution to site services, and new construction to rehabilitation projects. We deliver safely performed, high quality projects, on schedule. 01 02 Earthworks and Site Development Our team of dedicated professionals bring forward the ability to construct quality projects to the required standard of local Governments, Municipalities, Alberta Transportation, Alberta Environment and Parks, Alberta Infrastructure, First Nation communities, and have collaborated with these public bodies collectively and individually prior to joining Option. We’re proud to offer underground construction as well as the additional services of directional drilling, earthworks, general contracting, and/or construction management Canada-wide to clients. Storm Outfalls Our team’s experience in completing on-site and off-site trunk mains of varying sizes and depths, consists of and is not limited to • Large diameter PVC and concrete sanitary lines • Large diameter PVC and concrete storm main lines • Large diameter water distribution systems • Trenchless trunk main installations • Stormwater outfalls • Oil/grit separation units • Lift stations 03 04 Pipeline Construction From HDPE Transmission mains, sewer force mains and steel pipeline construction, our team has installed hundreds of KM’s of pipeline in western Canada over the last 5 years. Pipe Fusion Services Option’s in-house HDPE/PVC certified and experienced Fusion Technicians work with all diameters of pipe and are also certified for electrofusion couplers on HDPE. Their work is fully documented for quality assurance/control requirements and records are submitted throughout the duration of fusing activities. 05 06 Horizontal Directional Drilling (HDD) Option’s team has worked on numerous trenchless projects in the past. As a General Contractor, our experience in horizontal directional drilling, case bores, pipe-jacking, micro-tunneling and direct pipe installation allows us to monitor and collaborate with our subcontractors to ensure the most effective use of time during these activities. Lift Stations/Pump Stations Our team’s experience in completing on-site and off-site trunk mains of varying sizes and depths, consists of and is not limited to • Large diameter PVC and concrete sanitary lines • Large diameter PVC and concrete storm main lines • Large diameter water distribution systems • Trenchless trunk main installations • Stormwater outfalls • Oil/grit separation units • Lift stations 07 08 Heavy Haul & Transportation We have in-house heavy haul and transportation for heavy equipment as well as tandem & end dump trucking and hauling. Site Development For greenfield civil construction projects, our team can act as a skilled GC to handle everything from your deep utilities, earthworks construction, gravel base work, and all finish site works including paving, concrete and landscaping. Our team has experience developing private, residential and industrial land sections. 09 10 Design Build Projects Option Excavating has in house design engineering capabilities to partner with clients on design build opportunities including: Deep utilities, earthworks and roadbuilding, lift stations and pump stations, transmission pipelines and full site developments. Major Earthworks Projects Option Excavating has the team and equipment to handle any bulk earthworks projects from small site grading to full scale 1 Million cubic meter projects. From Rip Rap, to final base work we can cover every scope needed to bring your project full circle. 11 12 Hydrovac Our in house fleet of Hydrovac and straight vac trucks service our projects from HDD mud disposal, to delicate high pressure line locating. Demolition/Remediation Our team has tackled projects that involve contaminated soil remediation, and structural demolition. Our team and our equipment are suited to what each project requires. 13 14 Specialized Projects We have not only the experience and equipment to tackle the weird and different in civil construction, we actually have modeled our business on the desire to tackle the challenging and risky projects that other Civil Construction firms steer clear of. From individual bids ranging up to $70,000,000.00 we will tackle the toughest of them as your trusted Civil GC Contractor. With a proven track record of working with consultants and owners to suggest unique construction methods to tackle unique issues, we can handle anything you want to throw at us. Lift Stations, Pump Stations & Mechanical Our team has experience in retrofitting and installing new pre-cast lift stations and wet wells. At any depth our experienced team can GC any lift station project, including all the required mechanical and electrical works. H2S and confined Space required? No problem, we have access to any specialized entry equipment and procedures to complete work safety. 15 Let's start your project today Let's turn your dream project into a reality! With our expertise and passion, we are committed to bringing your ideas to life. Contact us today and let's get started on making your vision a tangible success. Together, we can create something extraordinary. Contact Us

title: About
headings: H2: Let's start your project today
text: Who We Are About Who we are About Our Company Option Excavating Inc. (“Option”) is a full-service underground infrastructure installation company, located in Spruce Grove, Alberta. We are a solely-owned corporation, specializing in installing water, sanitary sewer and storm sewer main lines and services, and strive to provide our clients with safe, accurate and efficient installations. Option’s beginnings successfully encompassed decades of underground work performed within the City of Edmonton and surrounding areas but in 2018 shifted the company’s direction to an under-served niche market within the petroleum/construction industry, effectively closing the underground division, and moving all manpower and resources to the new endeavour. The decision to pivot into a new market has been a rewarding and successful one. 2011 ESTABLISHED In 2022, Option found itself looking to diversify and reopened its the underground infrastructure division - recruiting a well-respected and experienced team led by our General Manager, who brings forward extensive construction and management knowledge through 20+ years of advancement in the industry, from field work to project management, estimating and operations management. Our team of dedicated professionals bring forward the ability to construct quality projects to the required standard of local Governments, Municipalities, Alberta Transportation, Alberta Environment and Parks, Alberta Infrastructure, First Nation communities, and have collaborated with these public bodies collectively and individually prior to joining Option. We’re proud to offer underground construction as well as the additional services of directional drilling, earthworks, general contracting, and/or construction management Canada-wide to clients. Option has been excited to re-enter the underground infrastructure market with this team and have successfully completed many projects in Alberta and Saskatchewan to date. Included in our work, we have partnered with two First Nation communities to provide safe drinking water to their residents and engaged in a pilot program to transparently measure and report economic benefits to their respective Nation through their project sponsorship. The Pehta reporting tool shows great promise in providing credibility, confidence and comparability in the selection of service providers for First Nations work, and Option embraced the opportunity to be an early adopter of this groundbreaking endeavour. Let's start your project today Let's turn your dream project into a reality! With our expertise and passion, we are committed to bringing your ideas to life. Contact us today and let's get started on making your vision a tangible success. Together, we can create something extraordinary. Contact Us Meet the team Our Team Johan Kruithof General Manager Jessie Lecocq Operations Manager Brad Buffalo Civil Construction Manager Brandy Braun Civil Project Manager Ashley Trembley HSE & HR Manager Steven Beath Underground Superintendent Tyler Cust Transportation Manager

title: Home
headings: H4: General and Emergency Repair Services
H2: Let's start your project today
text: BUILT FOR WESTERN CANADA Playing in the dirt, and thriving in the dirt - we don't chase easy work, we take on the tough risky civil construction jobs, get our hands dirty, and build lasting partnerships. We do all that in our play ground of Western Canada, with an amazing Team.  BE GOOD HUMANS We believe great companies are built by great people. At Option Excavating, we treat our employees, partners, clients, and communities with dignity, respect, and integrity. We value hard work, provide fair opportunities for growth, spare no expense to keep our team coming home to their families, and recognize the people who help build our success every day. We strive to create a culture where people are proud to work, contribute, and succeed together.  PASSIONATE FOR CIVIL Civil construction is what we do — and what we love. From underground utilities and earthworks to road building and community infrastructure, we take pride in building the foundations that communities depend on. Whether we are developing new neighbourhoods or rehabilitating existing infrastructure, we bring passion, quality, and experience to every project we undertake.  TRUST Trust is earned through actions, not words. We build trust by being transparent, delivering quality workmanship, working safe, standing behind our commitments, and building lasting relationships with our employees, clients, consultants, and partners. Our goal is to be a company people can rely on — in the office, in the field, and on every project we deliver.  NO FEAR We are built to take on the tough projects. Option Excavating embraces the challenging civil construction work that others may shy away from — technically complex projects, high-risk environments, large-scale infrastructure, and design-build solutions. From estimating and planning through execution in the field, we bring confidence, adaptability, and problem-solving to every challenge. We don’t avoid difficult projects — we pursue them, we execute them and we close them. 2011 FOUNDED IN Who we are About Option Excavating Inc. Option Excavating Inc. (Option) is a full-service underground infrastructure installation company, located in Spruce Grove, Alberta serving all of Western Canada. We specialize in installing water, sanitary sewer and storm sewer main lines and services,and strive to provide our clients with safe, accurate and efficient installations. Option’s beginnings successfully encompassed decades of underground work performed within the City of Edmonton and surrounding areas but in 2018 shifted the company’s direction to an under-served niche market within the petroleum/construction industry, effectively closing the underground division, and moving all manpower and resources to the new endeavour. The decision to pivot into a new market has been a rewarding and successful one. Read More What we offer Our Services Option is a full-service underground infrastructure installation company. We specialize in water, sanitary sewer and storm sewer main line installations and services. Our clients vary from municipal and provincial governments, and petroleum, commercial and residential sectors.  Urban Land Development We take pride in constructing communities, often where we live and work. Our experienced construction team, backed with the right equipment, allows us to execute a wide variety and scale of project – any size, type and depth of pipe can be addressed, from deep trunk lines to large diameter concrete storm and box  Municipal Rehabilitation As material lifecycles end, the need to replace existing infrastructure is has become a substantial component of the municipal construction industry. This work often includes water, sanitary and stormwater mains, fire hydrants, manholes, catch basins and services (including temporary potable water and sanitary bypasses).  Trenchless Construction (HDD, Pipe-Jack, Micro-tunneling, Case Bore, Direct Pipe Installation) Option’s team has worked on numerous trenchless projects in the past. As a General Contractor, our experience in horizontal directional drilling, case bores, pipe-jacking, micro-tunneling and direct pipe installation allows us to monitor and collaborate with our subcontractors to ensure the most effective use of time during these activities.  General and Emergency Repair Services Our team is well versed in regularly scheduled and emergency repair work of deep infrastructure. Repairs addressed have encompassed water main breaks, valve replacements, and sewer repairs to lesser and greater degrees.  Maintenance Services (Including Testing, Pigging and Temporary Water) Preventative maintenance is critical in maintaining the integrity of deep utility infrastructure and ensuring lifecycle expectations are achieved. Our team is eager to collaborate with clients to design and/or implement preventative programs to address their systems to ensure they remain in peak operation condition.  Pipe Fusion Services Option’s in-house HDPE/PVC certified and experienced Fusion Technicians work with all diameters of pipe and are also certified for electrofusion couplers on HDPE. Their work is fully documented for quality assurance/control requirements and records are submitted throughout the duration of fusing activities. View All Services Process Option’s Project Approach Option takes pride in delivering a quality project to our clientsand expanding our portfolio of work completed and services offered. We are continually building upon our good reputation in the industry as backed by our safety record, solid relationships within the engineering community and client references attesting to our efforts. Our field team has been together for years and have decades of combined experience guiding their approach on each project -ensuring the project is thoughtfully planned/phased prior to its start to maintain safe practices throughout the work. Discover Our Mission, Vision, and Core Values What we do Our Project Portfolio Let's start your project today Let's turn your dream project into a reality! With our expertise and passion, we are committed to bringing your ideas to life. Contact us today and let's get started on making your vision a tangible success. Together, we can create something extraordinary.
